title: Make session exit resumable; move 5 of 8 ops out of exit into mid-session or cron
current_state: agent/session.py::on_exit runs 8 ops sequentially: flush_logs → session_summary → distill L1→L2 → promote L2→L3 → prune L3 expired → prune L2 stale → weekly_audit (gated) → vault_sync. Each wrapped try/except 'non-fatal'. Mid-session distillation (T-072) partially covers L1→L2 but everything else is exit-only. SIGKILL or daemon crash mid-exit loses: session summary, vault sync, audit, prune work, L2→L3 promotion. The last one is the worst — a high-importance fact written in this session won't be in tomorrow's ambient L3 context.
structural_problem: Exit-heavy workflows assume clean shutdown. Real systems crash. Each non-fatal try/except masks the cumulative loss. No resumption logic means an unclean exit silently degrades memory consistency.
target_state: on_exit reduced to 3 ops: flush_logs → session_summary → write exit_state.json marker. The other 5 ops run mid-session (L2→L3, vault sync) or on cron (audit, prunes). On daemon startup, check data/session_exit_state.json — if last exit incomplete, finish remaining steps before accepting connections.
files_affected: agent/session.py (refactor on_exit into 3 named steps; add resumable state file writes)
pi_daemon.py (on startup: check data/session_exit_state.json; complete unfinished steps before listen)
pi_agent.py (_maybe_mid_session_distill gains L2→L3 promotion call after distillation)
tools/tools_obsidian.py (vault sync becomes mid-session-callable; per S-056 better_future_fix)
scripts/passive/memory_prune.py (new; replaces in-exit L2/L3 prune; runs daily)
success_criteria: Test: kill -9 daemon mid-exit; restart; verify remaining exit steps complete before first chat accepted
Test: write a high-importance L2 fact mid-session; without exit, verify L3 promotion happens within N turns
scripts/passive/ contains weekly_memory_audit.py and memory_prune.py registered on cron
data/session_exit_state.json schema documented in agent/session.py module docstring
risk_notes: Mid-session promotion + vault sync run in background threads. Use existing _async_log queue pattern + ensure idempotency (multiple promotions of same L2 fact must be safe — temporal validity window from S-054 makes this true).
migration_plan: 2. Define data/session_exit_state.json schema: {session_id, started_at, steps: [{name, status, completed_at}]}
3. Add writer in agent/session.py that records step progress; refactor on_exit to update state after each step
4. Add startup check in pi_daemon.py::_get_agent: read exit_state, if incomplete call resume_exit(state)
5. Move L2→L3 promotion from on_exit into pi_agent.py::_maybe_mid_session_distill (after distill_session call)
6. Move vault_sync out of on_exit; hook into _maybe_mid_session_distill for L2/L3 vault writes, leave L1/ticket vault sync at exit (cheap)
7. Extract weekly audit into scripts/passive/weekly_memory_audit.py with should_run_weekly gate moved into the script
8. Extract L2/L3 prunes into scripts/passive/memory_prune.py running daily
9. Trim on_exit to flush_logs → session_summary → finalize exit_state
